name: triggers
context: Match natural-language phrases to Dojo nodes for intent-based routing
info: Trigger matching routes natural-language requests to the most relevant Dojo node. When an agent receives a task like 'deploy my app to vercel' or 'create a github issue', trigger matching compares the input against every node's `triggers[]` array using semantic similarity scoring. Triggers carry the highest search weight (1.0) because they represent direct intent matches — the node author has explicitly declared that this phrase means this node. Trigger matching is the primary routing mechanism for agent-to-graph intent mapping.
triggers: how do triggers work in dojo
route a task to a dojo node
body:
# Trigger Matching

Triggers are the highest-signal discovery mechanism in Dojo. They represent direct intent declarations: the node author says 'when someone says this phrase, they mean this node.'

## How It Works

1. Agent receives a task: `create a github issue for the login bug`
2. Trigger engine tokenizes and normalizes the input
3. Each node's `triggers[]` array is scored against the input
4. Scoring uses token overlap + order weighting + coverage ratio
5. Top-scoring node is returned as the primary match

## Defining Triggers

Triggers go in the `triggers[]` field of `node.json`:

```json
{
  "name": "issues",
  "uri": "github/issues",
  "triggers": [
    "create a github issue",
    "file a bug report",
    "search github issues",
    "label an issue",
    "triage issues",
    "manage milestones"
  ]
}
```

### Best Practices

- Write triggers as complete phrases an agent would actually receive
- Include verb + object patterns: `create a repo`, `deploy to vercel`, `run tests`
- Cover the main actions the node supports
- Vary the phrasing: `create an issue`, `file a bug`, `open a ticket`
- Don't duplicate triggers across nodes — each phrase should map to exactly one node
- Don't use generic phrases like `help` or `do something` — they match everything

## Scoring Algorithm

Trigger scoring combines three signals:

| Signal | Weight | Description |
|--------|--------|-----------|
| Token overlap | 0.5 | How many trigger tokens appear in the input |
| Order weighting | 0.3 | Whether matching tokens appear in the same order |
| Coverage ratio | 0.2 | What fraction of the trigger is covered by the input |

A trigger scores 1.0 when every token matches in order. The minimum threshold for a trigger match is 0.6.

## Trigger vs. Alias

| | Triggers | Aliases |
|---|---------|----------|
| Purpose | Route tasks to nodes | Resolve names to URIs |
| Format | Complete phrases | Single terms or short names |
| Weight | 1.0 (highest) | 0.9 |
| Example | `deploy to vercel` | `vercel deploy` |
| Use case | Agent receives a task | Agent knows a name variant |

## Environment Variables

| Variable | Purpose |
|----------|----------|
| `DOJO_URL` | Base URL of the Dojo registry |

sections.title: Defining triggers
sections.body: Triggers are declared in the `triggers[]` array of `node.json`. Each trigger is a complete natural-language phrase that an agent would receive as a task. Use verb-object patterns like `create a repo`, `deploy to vercel`, `run tests`. Vary phrasing to cover different ways agents express the same intent. Avoid generic phrases that match too broadly.

sections.title: Scoring algorithm
sections.body: Trigger scoring combines token overlap (0.5 weight), order weighting (0.3), and coverage ratio (0.2). A perfect match scores 1.0 — all tokens match in order with full coverage. The minimum threshold for a trigger match is 0.6. Below that threshold, the search falls back to alias resolution and text search. Ties are broken by node depth (shallower wins).

sections.title: Triggers vs aliases
sections.body: Triggers and aliases serve different purposes. Triggers route complete task phrases to nodes — they answer 'what should I do with this request?' Aliases resolve name variants to URIs — they answer 'what is this thing called?' Triggers use phrase-level matching; aliases use term-level matching. Both are indexed at search time but triggers carry the highest weight (1.0 vs 0.9). See [[dojo/search/aliases]] for alias resolution details.
